titleInfo.title: Foshuo guanding qiwan erqian shenwang hu biqiu zhou jing
佛說灌頂七萬二千神王護比丘咒經
Dhāraṇī Sutra Spoken by the Buddha on the Consecration of the Seventy-two thousand Spirit Kings Protecting the Bhikṣu

mods.abstract: Strickman 2002, Chinese Magical Medicine, 132-140; and Strickman 1990, The Consecration Sūtra: A Buddhist Book of Spells. E. Zürcher Perspectives in the Study of Chinese Buddhism. Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, no. 1 (1982), 161-176, on page 168. "According to its introductory section, 600 years after the Parinirvāna, when the world will be steeped in sin, there will be a pious monk named P'u-chi 普濟 who practices dhyāna in various mountain caves. On one of his peregrinations he will discover a beautiful grotto, and there he will find a precious box containing a copy of this scripture, written with gold script on finely carved slips of sandalwood. He will ador this divine text and propagate it again in the world, that all may be saved. It will be a potent weapon against all evil powers and dangers. The text itself will act as a protective charm; even for those who cannot read: they should carry it in a silk pouch on the chest or at the girdle, and all evil spirits will be powerless. However, much resisitance is to be expected, for many traditionalist monks will reject it as spurious and heretical, with terrible karmic consequences to themselves." Strickmann 1990, 82-83 gives a summary of the contents of the twelfth chapter, "Eliminating Faults and Transcending Life-and-Death". Strickmann 1990, 96 and 97, translates a short passage from the eleventh chapter.

originInfo.dateOther: translated between 317-322; composed near Nanjing in 457 (Strickmann 1982, 57)

titleInfo.title: New edition of the Buddhist canon in the Taishō era
